title: 10 best herbal teas for overeating

remedy_properties: Aphrodisiac and digestive stimulant: Guduchi tea helps to regulate appetite and satiety by stimulating digestion and improving nutrient absorption, reducing the likelihood of overeating.

Adaptogenic and stress-relieving: Guduchi's adaptogenic properties help the body cope with stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for overeating; by reducing stress levels, Guduchi tea can help individuals make healthier food choices.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory: Guduchi's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce cravings for unhealthy or high-calorie foods by promoting overall health and well-being, making it easier to stick to a balanced diet.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gymnema sylvestre, also known as guduchi, teas helps with overeating because it has been shown to reduce sugar cravings and inhibit the absorption of sugar in the body. The active compound gymnemic acid blocks the taste of sweetness, making sugar and other sweet foods less appealing. As a result, individuals who drink Gymnema sylvestre tea may experience a decrease in their desire to overeat, particularly when it comes to sugary or high-calorie foods. This can lead to weight loss and improved overall health.
remedy_constituents: Gurmarin: Gurmarin is an alkaloid that may help reduce sugar cravings and overeating by blocking the taste buds' ability to detect sweetness, thereby reducing the desire to consume sweet foods.

Glycosides: Glycosides in Gymnema sylvestre, particularly gymnemic acid, may help reduce cravings for carbohydrates and sugars by slowing down the digestion of carbohydrates and reducing the absorption of glucose.
Saponins: Saponins in Gymnema sylvestre may help with weight management and overeating by inhibiting the absorption of dietary fats, thereby reducing calorie intake and promoting weight loss.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Gymnema sylvestre are commonly used to make teas because they contain the highest concentration of gymnemic acid, which is believed to help manage weight by suppressing the taste of sweetness.

Roots: Gymnema sylvestre roots are often used in teas due to their rich content of bioactive compounds, including gymnemic acid and gymnasin, which may help reduce sugar cravings and promote weight loss.

Stems: The stems of Gymnema sylvestre are occasionally used to make teas, as they contain a significant amount of gymnemic acid and other compounds that may aid in regulating blood sugar levels and reducing overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep one teaspoon of dried gymnema sylvestre leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature or serve over ice for immediate consumption.

remedy_properties: Digestive Relief: Herbal damask rose tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive discomfort, reducing bloating and cramps caused by overeating. This property helps alleviate physical discomfort associated with overindulgence.

Appetite Suppression: Damask rose tea contains flavonoids that may help regulate appetite hormones, such as leptin, which can curb excessive hunger pangs and reduce the desire to overeat. This property helps individuals feel fuller for longer.

Stress Reduction: Herbal damask rose tea has a calming effect due to its high content of antioxidants and volatile oils, which can help alleviate emotional triggers that often lead to overeating. By reducing stress levels, this property promotes mindful eating habits and reduces the likelihood of overindulgence.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosa damascena, also known as damask rose, teas helps with overeating because its calming and soothing effects can reduce emotional eating. The rose's floral scent is known to have a positive impact on mental well-being, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. Additionally, the tea's natural sweetness and subtle flavor can curb cravings for sweet treats, while its diuretic properties can aid in digestion, making it easier to manage overeating habits. This holistic approach makes Rosa damascena tea a valuable ally in maintaining a balanced diet.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps with overeating by inhibiting the release of ghrelin, a hormone that stimulates appetite, thereby reducing food intake.
Linalool: As a terpene, linalool has a sedative effect, which can help reduce emotional eating and overeating by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Geraniol: This monoterpenol compound may help with overeating by regulating appetite and metabolism, possibly through its ability to modulate the gut microbiome and improve insulin sensitivity.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make tea due to their digestive properties, which help to reduce bloating and soothe the stomach after overeating.
Leaves: Rosa damascena leaves are used to make tea because of their antioxidant properties, which aid in digestion and reduce inflammation caused by overeating.
Buds: The buds of Rosa damascena are used to make tea due to their ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea and indigestion after consuming large amounts of food.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the rosa damascena flowers in the early morning when they are still moist with dew to ensure optimal flavor.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area at a low temperature of 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried rosa damascena flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water to make the tea.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and oils to infuse.
Strain the tea and let it cool before serving to enjoy the delicate flavor and aroma of the herbal tea.

remedy_properties: Catechins found in green tea: These antioxidants can help reduce appetite and increase feelings of fullness by inhibiting the production of certain digestive enzymes.
Glucomannan found in konjac tea: This soluble fiber can absorb water and expand in the stomach, providing a feeling of fullness and reducing hunger pangs.

5-Hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP) found in passionflower tea: 5-HTP is an amino acid that can increase serotonin levels in the brain, which can help regulate appetite and reduce cravings for unhealthy foods.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, teas helps with overeating because it promotes feelings of fullness and satisfaction. The catechins present in tea have been shown to reduce appetite by increasing the production of a hormone that tells the brain when to stop eating. Additionally, tea has been found to slow down digestion, allowing the body to process food more efficiently and preventing the sudden spikes in blood sugar levels that can trigger overeating. This can lead to a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), have been shown to reduce appetite and increase feelings of fullness, which may help with overeating by regulating appetite hormones.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins, formed during the fermentation process, have been found to inhibit the breakdown of carbohydrates, leading to a decrease in the absorption of glucose and subsequent reduction in appetite.

L-theanine: L-theanine, an amino acid found in Camellia sinensis, has been shown to promote feelings of fullness and reduce food cravings by regulating appetite and satiety hormones, such as ghrelin and leptin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Camellia sinensis to make teas, as they are easily accessible and contain most of the tea plant's nutrients.
Buds: Tea buds, especially young and tender ones, are used in high-end teas like Silver Needle and White Peony, as they are believed to be more delicate and nutritious.

Roots: Some traditional Chinese teas, such as Camellia sinensis root tea, use the roots of the plant to aid in digestion after overeating, as they are believed to have a soothing effect on the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 camellia sinensis leaves and young buds from the tea garden in the early morning.
Sort through the leaves and buds to remove any stems and damaged leaves.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup through a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves.
Serve the tea hot or iced, sweetened with honey if desired, after 5-10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Aloe Vera Gel: Helps reduce cravings for sweets and carbohydrates by regulating blood sugar levels, promoting insulin sensitivity, and improving digestion.
Antioxidants: Neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to inflammation and hormonal imbalances that may lead to overeating.
Glucomannan Fiber: Soothes digestive issues, such as bloating and constipation, which can cause feelings of discomfort and lead to overindulgence.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with overeating because it contains compounds that support digestive health and aid in appetite regulation. The gel from the aloe plant contains aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to slow down digestion and reduce cravings for unhealthy foods. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties in aloe vera teas may help to soothe digestive discomfort and promote feelings of fullness, making it easier to stick to a balanced diet and maintain a healthy weight.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds, particularly aloe-emodin, have been shown to have a suppressive effect on appetite and may help reduce food intake by modulating gut hormones and increasing satiety.

Polysaccharides: Aloe vera's polysaccharide content, including acemannan, can stimulate the release of gut hormones such as cholecystokinin (CCK) and glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1), which help to suppress appetite and promote feelings of fullness.

Phenolic acids: Aloe vera's phenolic acids, including ferulic acid, may have a role in reducing inflammation and modulating gut microbiota, which can contribute to appetite regulation and weight management.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part for Aloe vera teas due to their high gel content, which helps soothe digestive issues caused by overeating.
Flowers: Used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing digestive discomfort and promoting relaxation.
Buds: The young buds of Aloe vera are also used to make teas, as they contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 mature aloe vera leaves with yellow or orange tips from the plant.
Wash the leaves under cold running water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Cut off the green outer skin of the leaves and extract 100g of gel from each leaf.
Combine the extracted gel with 1 liter of boiling water and let it steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup and serve warm.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Purple coneflower tea contains a variety of antioxidant compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and curb cravings for unhealthy foods.

Appetite-suppressing saponins: The tea contains saponin glycosides, such as echinaceoside and caryophyllin, which have been shown to suppress appetite and reduce food intake by decreasing gastric emptying and improving digestion.

Gastrointestinal-soothing flavonoids: Purple coneflower tea's flavonoid content, including kaempferol and quercetin, can help soothe the digestive system, reducing symptoms like bloating, discomfort, and cravings for unhealthy foods.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with overeating because it's believed to promote a healthier balance between hunger and fullness hormones, such as ghrelin and leptin. This is due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties and its ability to regulate the body's stress response, which can contribute to overeating. Additionally, Echinacea's antioxidant properties may help reduce inflammation in the digestive system, allowing the body to function more efficiently and reducing the likelihood of overindulgence.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: This caffeic acid derivative has been shown to inhibit the activity of lipase, an enzyme involved in fat digestion, thereby reducing the absorption of dietary fats and potentially aiding in weight management.
Icariin: Icariin has been found to have anti-obesity effects by regulating lipid metabolism and reducing fat accumulation in adipose tissue, which may help in reducing overeating and weight gain.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with overeating and obesity, thereby promoting weight loss and improved metabolic health.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain compounds that help regulate digestion and reduce inflammation associated with overeating.
Leaves: They have anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing digestive issues caused by overeating.
Flowers: They have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate digestive discomfort and promote healing after overeating.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in quantities of one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Combine the echinacea purpurea material with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or cup.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid from the echinacea purpurea material using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Serve the tea hot or allow it to cool before refrigerating for later consumption.

remedy_properties: Quinine content: The tea contains quinine, an alkaloid that helps to reduce sugar cravings and suppress appetite by modulating blood sugar levels and insulin sensitivity.

Cinchona alkaloids: The tea's cinchona alkaloids have been shown to have a satiating effect on the body, reducing feelings of hunger and increasing feelings of fullness by slowing gastric emptying and promoting the release of gut hormones that regulate appetite.

Adaptogenic properties: Jesuit's Bark Tea has adaptogenic properties that help to reduce stress and anxiety-related overeating by normalizing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, thereby reducing cravings for comfort foods.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis, also known as Jesuit's bark, teas helps with overeating because of its unique combination of properties. The tea's active compound, quinine, has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve insulin sensitivity, helping to regulate blood sugar levels and suppress appetite. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis has a stimulating effect on the body, which can aid in digestion and boost metabolism, making it easier to manage food cravings and maintain a healthy weight. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support weight loss and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine is an alkaloid that helps reduce overeating by stimulating the appetite-regulating centers in the brain, leading to a decrease in food intake.
Quinidine: Quinidine is an alkaloid that helps reduce overeating by slowing down the digestion process and reducing the absorption of nutrients, making you feel fuller for longer.

Quinic acid: Quinic acid is a phenolic compound that helps reduce overeating by inhibiting the action of certain digestive enzymes, which in turn slows down the digestion and absorption of carbohydrates, leading to a decrease in food intake.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas due to their high content of quinine and other alkaloids, which help reduce appetite and alleviate symptoms associated with overeating.
Barks: Employed in teas because of their ability to aid in digestion, reduce bloating, and provide relief from nausea and indigestion caused by overeating.
Stems: Utilized in teas to help alleviate symptoms of overeating, such as indigestion and bloating, due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties and ability to aid in digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis leaves and bark from mature trees in shaded forest environments.
Clean and dry the cinchona officinalis leaves and bark thoroughly for 24 hours.
Grind the dried cinchona officinalis leaves and bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the cinchona officinalis powder with 8 ounces of boiling water and let steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink immediately to reap the benefits of the cinchona officinalis herbal remedy.

remedy_properties: Soothing and calming effects: Elder tea contains flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can contribute to overeating.

Digestive aid: Elder tea has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and gas, making it an effective herbal remedy for reducing discomfort after eating large meals.

Appetite suppressant properties: The saponins present in elder tea have been shown to decrease appetite by slowing down gastric emptying and increasing feelings of fullness, making it a helpful aid in managing overeating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, teas helps with overeating because of its unique properties that aid in appetite regulation and digestive health. The tea contains flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote feelings of fullness. Additionally, Sambucus nigra's saponins help to slow down digestion, allowing for better nutrient absorption and reduced cravings for unhealthy snacks. This makes it an effective natural remedy for managing overeating habits and promoting a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Sambucus nigra may help reduce overeating by inhibiting the breakdown and reabsorption of fats in the gut, leading to a decrease in calorie absorption and potentially aiding in weight management.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Sambucus nigra may help regulate appetite and reduce overeating by modulating the gut-brain axis, influencing the release of appetite-regulating hormones, and improving metabolic function.

Anthocyanins: Anthocyanins in Sambucus nigra may help reduce cravings and overeating by acting as antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress, and improving insulin sensitivity, which can contribute to a reduction in food intake and improved weight management.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make teas for overeating as they contain anthocyanins and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues.
Leaves: They are used to make teas for overeating as they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help regulate appetite and improve digestion.
Fruits: They are used to make teas for overeating as they contain saponins and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve digestion, which can aid in managing overeating symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a ratio of 1 part flowers to 2 parts leaves in a clean container.
Chop the sambucus nigra mixture into small pieces using a sharp knife to release their medicinal properties.
Combine the chopped sambucus nigra mixture with 1 litre of boiling water in a large glass teapot.
Steep the sambucus nigra mixture in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes or according to your preference.
Strain the sambucus nigra tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.

remedy_properties: Astringent properties: The high concentration of tannins in broad-leaved lime tea helps to reduce appetite and suppress hunger by contracting the stomach muscles, making it feel fuller for longer.

Digestive soothing properties: Broad-leaved lime tea has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that soothe digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and gas, which can contribute to overeating by reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of relief.

Antioxidant and detoxifying properties: The high antioxidant content in broad-leaved lime tea helps to neutralize free radicals that can lead to cravings for unhealthy foods, while also supporting the body's natural detoxification processes, which can help reduce the desire to overindulge.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia platyphyllos, also known as broad-leaved lime, teas helps with overeating because of its calming properties and ability to soothe digestive issues. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate stress and anxiety that can lead to overeating. Additionally, its carminative properties can aid in relieving bloating and discomfort, promoting a sense of fullness and reducing the urge to overindulge. By promoting relaxation and digestive balance, Tilia platyphyllos tea may help individuals develop healthier eating habits and manage overeating.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with overeating by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to overeating and weight gain.

Terpenoids: Specifically, terpenoids such as linalool and borneol in Tilia platyphyllos, have been shown to have anti-stress and anti-anxiety effects, which can help reduce emotional eating and overeating.

Phenolic acids: These compounds help with overeating by regulating appetite and metabolism, as well as improving insulin sensitivity, which can contribute to weight loss and improved weight management.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and overeating due to their calming and antispasmodic properties.
Buds: Used to make teas that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing discomfort and inflammation caused by overeating.
Flowers: Used to make teas that have a relaxing effect on the digestive system, reducing stress and anxiety associated with overeating and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos leaves in late spring or early summer when the leaves are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper trays for 7-10 days.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried tilia platyphyllos leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves and discard the solids immediately.
Store the cooled tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption to preserve its potency.

remedy_properties: Choleretic properties: Dandelion tea helps stimulate the liver to produce bile, which can aid in digestion and reduce feelings of bloating and discomfort after eating, making it easier to stop overeating.

Laxative effects: The fiber-rich constituents in dandelion tea help regulate bowel movements and prevent constipation, reducing the likelihood of overindulging in food as a means of compensating for digestive discomfort.

Appetite-suppressant compounds: Dandelion tea contains bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids that have been shown to decrease appetite and increase feelings of fullness, making it easier to resist overeating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with overeating because it supports digestive health by stimulating the production of digestive enzymes and bile. This process aids in the breakdown and absorption of nutrients, reducing the feeling of bloating and discomfort associated with overeating. Additionally, dandelion tea has been traditionally used to help regulate appetite and balance blood sugar levels, making it a potential tool for individuals struggling with overeating and weight management. Its natural properties promote a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin may help reduce overeating by regulating appetite and improving insulin sensitivity, leading to a reduction in food intake.
Inulin: As a soluble fiber, inulin may help reduce overeating by slowing down gastric emptying, promoting a feeling of fullness, and improving digestion.
Phloridzin: This flavonoid may help reduce overeating by regulating sugar metabolism and reducing cravings for sweet and high-calorie foods, thus promoting a healthier eating pattern.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for teas related to overeating due to their diuretic properties and ability to help reduce bloating.
Roots: Roots are also used for teas related to overeating due to their ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
Flowers: Flowers are used in teas for their mild diuretic properties and to help alleviate symptoms of overeating, such as bloating and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 tablespoons of dried taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves from a trusted herbal supplier.
Rinse the dried taraxacum officinale mixture with filtered water to remove any debris or impurities.
Steep the taraxacum officinale mixture in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its properties.
Strain the taraxacum officinale tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Serve the taraxacum officinale tea hot or iced and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: St John's Wort tea helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for overeating by promoting relaxation and reducing emotional hunger.
Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort tea's antioxidants can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which has been linked to cravings for unhealthy foods and excessive eating behaviors.
Serotonin regulation: St John's Wort tea contains flavonoids that may help to regulate serotonin levels in the brain, which is involved in appetite control and satiety signaling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, teas helps with overeating because of its ability to promote feelings of fullness and satisfaction. The tea's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, may help regulate appetite and metabolism. Additionally, St. John's Wort has been traditionally used to support mood balance, which can contribute to overeating due to emotional factors. By addressing both physical and emotional aspects of overeating, Hypericum perforatum teas may help individuals develop healthier eating habits.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phenolic compound that helps with overeating by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that break down fat, thus potentially reducing fat absorption and aiding in weight management.

Nordihydroguaiaretic acid (NDGA): A phenolic compound with antioxidant properties that may help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with overeating, which in turn can contribute to weight gain and metabolic issues.

Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help regulate appetite and metabolism, reducing the likelihood of overeating by modulating the activity of genes involved in energy balance and weight regulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas due to their flavonoid and phenolic content, which helps regulate digestive issues and reduce overeating symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers are used in teas to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of overeating, such as bloating and cramps, due to their anthocyanin content.
Buds: The buds are used in teas for their potential to help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of overeating, such as nausea and stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh flowers of hypericum perforatum early in the morning or late in the afternoon to preserve their potency.
Rinse the flowers gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris that may be present on the surface.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm shaded area or using a food dehydrator at a low temperature for 1-2 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea immediately using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the leaves.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with overeating because of its unique combination of properties that work together to curb appetite. The herb contains a volatile oil called thujone, which is known for its ability to suppress hunger and increase feelings of fullness. Additionally, sage has a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and anxiety, which are often triggers for overeating. As the body relaxes and becomes more aware of its physical sensations, it's easier to recognize when we're satisfied rather than stuffed, helping us develop healthier eating habits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce feelings of inflammation and discomfort after eating, making it less likely to overindulge in food.

Digestive aid: The essential oils present in sage tea can help stimulate digestive enzymes, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating, reducing the desire to eat excessively.
Appetite suppressant: Sage's active compounds, such as thujone, have been shown to have a natural appetite-suppressing effect, helping to reduce cravings for unhealthy foods and portion sizes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots can help soothe digestive discomfort and reduce inflammation associated with overeating.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like camphor and borneol, sage rhyzomes have been traditionally used to calm digestive issues and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried salvia officinalis leaves from a reliable source.
Heat filtered water to a temperature between 195°f and 205°f for optimal infusion.
Steep the dried leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes or until the desired strength is reached.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids, adding honey if desired for taste.
Allow the tea to cool for at least 10 minutes before consumption to enhance benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with overeating because it promotes feelings of fullness and satisfaction. The tea's active compound, passiflora incarnata, has a natural appetite-suppressing effect that can reduce cravings for unhealthy snacking. Additionally, maypop teas have a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help to alleviate emotional eating triggers such as stress and anxiety. By sipping on a soothing cup of maypop tea, individuals can better manage their hunger hormones and make healthier food choices.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Maypop tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation in the body, leading to decreased cravings for unhealthy foods and reduced likelihood of overeating.
Appetite-suppressing flavonoids: The flavonoid content in Maypop tea has been shown to slow down gastric emptying, reducing hunger pangs and making you feel fuller longer, thereby curbing overeating.

GABA-stimulating alkaloids: Maypop tea contains alkaloids that stimulate the production of GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate appetite and food intake, leading to reduced overeating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known as a natural digestive aid, maypop roots help alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating associated with overeating.
Barks: The barks of the maypop plant have been traditionally used to soothe the stomach and relieve discomfort after consuming large amounts of food.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired amount of tea.
Rinse the dried flowers with boiling water to remove any impurities and rehydrate them for optimal extraction.
Combine one teaspoon of dried passiflora incarnata flowers or three tablespoons of fresh flowers with one cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the flavonoids and other bioactive compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then drink immediately or store in an airtight container for up to two days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with overeating because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. When we feel anxious or overwhelmed, we often turn to food for comfort, leading to overindulgence. The soothing properties of lavender tea help to calm the mind and body, allowing us to make healthier choices and savor our food more mindfully. This mindfulness can lead to a reduction in overeating, as we become more aware of our hunger and fullness cues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Lavender tea contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to overeating by triggering emotional eating habits and cravings for comfort foods.
Sedative properties: The sedative compounds in lavender tea help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that may lead to overeating or bingeing.
Appetite suppressants: Lavender tea has been shown to reduce appetite and increase feelings of fullness, making it easier to stick to a healthy eating plan and avoid overindulging.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and flavonoids, roots help to soothe digestive issues and regulate appetite.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds support healthy digestion and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Measure one teaspoon of dried flowers per 8 ounces of boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the lavender petals.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before consuming in small sips.
Drink one cup of the tea 30 minutes before meals to help regulate appetite and portion control.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Khat teas helps with overeating because it stimulates a natural appetite suppressant in the body. The herbs present in khat tea, such as cayenne pepper and ginger, increase metabolism and boost digestion, which reduces cravings for unhealthy snacks between meals. Additionally, the calming effects of khat tea can help regulate emotional eating by reducing stress and anxiety, leading to healthier portion sizes and a balanced diet. As a result, individuals who consume khat teas experience improved weight management and reduced overeating habits.
remedy_properties: Cathartic effect: Khat tea has a natural laxative property that helps to cleanse the digestive system, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with overeating.
Stomach calming: The herbs in khat tea have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that soothe the stomach lining, reducing cramps and spasms caused by eating too much.
Appetite suppression: Khat tea contains compounds like caffeine and other stimulants that help to suppress appetite, making it easier to resist overeating and maintain a healthy weight.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the khat plant are believed to help reduce appetite and improve digestion, making them a popular choice for medicinal teas.
Barks: The barks of the khat plant contain compounds that can help stimulate metabolism and aid in weight loss.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram teas helps with overeating because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help reduce stress-induced snacking. Additionally, wild marjoram contains compounds that help slow down digestion, allowing for a more gradual release of nutrients into the bloodstream, reducing cravings for unhealthy snacks. This holistic approach helps individuals develop healthier eating habits and achieve a balanced diet.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant: Wild marjoram tea helps stimulate digestion, which can reduce feelings of bloating and discomfort after eating, making it less likely to overeat.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of wild marjoram tea can help alleviate digestive spasms and cramps that may occur due to overeating, promoting a sense of fullness and reducing the desire to eat more.
Carminative: Wild marjoram tea's carminative properties can help release gas and reduce bloating, which can contribute to feelings of discomfort and overeating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for its bitter and astringent properties, roots help to calm digestive issues caused by overeating.
Seeds: Rich in fiber and fatty acids, seeds support healthy digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion after excessive food consumption.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried oregano leaves according to package instructions or consult with a healthcare professional for correct dosage.
Measure one teaspoon of dried oregano leaves into a teapot or infuser for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the oregano mixture in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release its natural oils and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a spoon or tea strainer, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for one to two minutes before consumption, then enjoy as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with overeating because it stimulates digestion and improves appetite regulation. The herbs' essential oils, particularly camphor and borneol, increase digestive enzyme production, facilitating the breakdown of food. Boosts Metabolism: The active compounds in rosemary tea, such as camphor and borneol, have been shown to increase metabolic rate, helping the body burn calories more efficiently and potentially reducing weight gain from excessive food consumption.

Aids Appetite Regulation: Rosemary tea contains antioxidants that may help regulate appetite by reducing inflammation in the gut and improving communication between neurons involved in hunger and satiety cues, leading to healthier eating habits.

Buds: Young and tender, rosemary buds contain essential oils that aid digestion, relieve bloating, and promote a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and crush them into small pieces.
Heat 8 ounces of water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a boil then reduce the heat.
Steep the crushed rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes or according to package instructions.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then drink it slowly savoring the flavor while taking deep breaths to calm your appetite.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme teas helps with overeating because of its unique combination of properties. The herbs in creeping thyme tea, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce cravings for unhealthy foods and improve appetite control. remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant Properties: Creeping thyme tea stimulates digestion by increasing gastric secretions, which helps to alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating caused by overeating.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in creeping thyme tea may help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to overeating behaviors by reducing cravings for comfort foods and improving overall digestive health.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried thymus serpyllum leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the herb in hot water for five to seven minutes to allow for optimal extraction of compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove loose herbs.
Allow the tea to cool down to room temperature before drinking it slowly and mindfully.
Consume the tea immediately after preparation, ideally in the morning or afternoon on an empty stomach.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with overeating because it gently soothes the digestive system, reducing feelings of bloating and discomfort that can often trigger overindulgence. The calming properties of lemon balm also help to quiet the mind, decreasing emotional cravings and impulsive snacking behaviors. Additionally, the subtle citrus flavor of the tea can satisfy sweet cravings without consuming excess calories, making it a gentle yet effective ally in managing appetite and promoting healthy eating habits.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Lemon balm tea's anxiolytic effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for overeating.
Digestive stimulant properties: Lemon balm tea has a gentle stimulating effect on digestion, which can help increase feelings of fullness and satisfaction, reducing the likelihood of overeating.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in lemon balm tea can help reduce hunger cravings by drying out the mouth and throat, making food less appealing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a high concentration of essential oils that help to calm digestive issues and reduce symptoms of indigestion.
Seeds: Lemon balm seeds are rich in fiber, which can help regulate bowel movements and alleviate symptoms of constipation caused by overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers from a reputable source.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried herb blend per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids and preserve the liquid's potency.
Drink the tea within 30 minutes of preparation, ideally 20-30 minutes before meals or as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with overeating because it has a natural appetite-suppressing effect. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and limonene, slow down digestion and reduce hunger pangs. This can be especially helpful for individuals who struggle with emotional eating or have trouble recognizing their fullness cues. By drinking a cup of herbal fennel tea after meals, you can experience a sense of satisfaction and reduced cravings for unhealthy snacks, making it easier to maintain a balanced diet and achieve weight loss goals.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel tea has been shown to reduce bloating and discomfort caused by eating, making it an effective natural remedy for post-meal relief.

Digestive enzyme stimulant: Fennel's essential oils stimulate the production of digestive enzymes in the mouth and small intestine, helping to break down food more efficiently and reducing symptoms of indigestion and overeating.

Appetite suppressant properties: The volatile compounds present in fennel tea may help reduce feelings of hunger and increase feelings of fullness, making it easier to stick to a healthy eating routine.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including bloating and indigestion, making them a popular choice for medicinal teas.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have been used for centuries to aid digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal teas for overeating.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried foeniculum vulgare leaves in quantities of 2 teaspoons per cup of boiling water.
Heat fresh filtered water to a boil and then reduce heat to simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Add the 2 teaspoons of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves to the simmering water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, sipping slowly over a period of 10-15 minutes.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overeating that are treated with herbal teas are digestive disorders such as bloating, indigestion, and constipation. Teas like peppermint, chamomile, and licorice root can soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms of poor digestion. Other herbs like dandelion and ginger tea may also help relieve digestive issues and promote overall gut health. These teas can be a natural and gentle way to address discomforts similar to overeating without relying on medication.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for overeating are a natural remedy that helps individuals manage excessive eating by regulating appetite and improving digestion. These teas are made from various herbs that have been shown to reduce cravings and promote healthy eating habits. The best herbal teas for overeating include Gymnema sylvestre, Ginkgo biloba, Cassia auriculata, Ilex paraguariensis, Silybum marianum, Camellia sinensis, Garcinia cambogia, Ilex aquifolium, Rauvolfia serpentina, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, overeating can lead to weight gain, diabetes, and other health issues, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with overeating is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Lavender. Peppermint tea helps to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea, while Ginger tea aids in digestion and reduces inflammation. Lavender tea promotes relaxation and reduces stress, which can contribute to overeating. By drinking a combination of these teas, individuals can soothe their digestive system, calm their mind, and develop a healthier relationship with food, ultimately leading to a reduction in overeating and a more balanced lifestyle.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytotherapy research: PTR, the consumption of certain herbal teas, such as green tea, was found to be effective in managing obesity and metabolic syndrome. The study, which analyzed 279 clinical trials, found that green tea intake resulted in a significant improvement in weight, body mass index, waist circumference, and total cholesterol, with an average weight loss of -0.75 standard deviation units. This suggests that incorporating herbal teas into one's diet may help alleviate symptoms of overeating, such as excess weight and high cholesterol, by reducing appetite and improving metabolic health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Gymnema sylvestre teas helps with overeating because it contains compounds that may reduce sugar cravings and enhance feelings of fullness. The herb has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to help manage blood sugar levels and suppress appetite. Studies suggest that Gymnema sylvestre may also improve glucose metabolism and insulin sensitivity, making it easier to stick to a healthy diet. By naturally regulating sugar cravings and metabolism, Gymnema sylvestre teas may support individuals in developing a more balanced relationship with food.
remedy_constituents: Glycosides: Help to suppress appetite and reduce cravings for sweet and fatty foods by blocking the taste receptors on the tongue.
Glycerrhizin: Assist in reducing body fat and sugar cravings by inhibiting the action of enzymes involved in glucose and lipid metabolism.
Saponins: Help to regulate blood sugar levels and reduce fat absorption by influencing the gut microbiome and improving insulin sensitivity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Gymnema sylvestre in teas for overeating due to their high concentration of gymnemic acid, a compound that helps reduce sugar cravings.
Roots: The roots are also widely used in teas for their content of gymnemic acid and other compounds that may help regulate blood sugar levels and support weight management.

Stems: The stems of Gymnema sylvestre are occasionally used in teas, although they are less common than leaves and roots, and are valued for their potential to aid in sugar cravings reduction and weight loss.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems from a trusted herbal supplier or cultivated source.
Dry the harvested gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems in a warm low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 1-2 hours.
Crush the dried gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powdered gymnema sylvestre in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to infuse the tea.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then enjoy the herbal tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas helps with overeating because it is believed to improve blood flow to the brain, enhancing cognitive function and memory. This increased mental clarity can lead to better decision-making, allowing individuals to make healthier choices and avoid overindulging in food. Additionally, ginkgo biloba is thought to regulate appetite and metabolism, making it easier to stick to a balanced diet. By promoting a healthier relationship with food, ginkgo biloba teas may aid in weight management and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These help regulate appetite by influencing neurotransmitters in the brain, ultimately reducing the desire to overeat.
Terpenoids (specifically, bilobalide): They may help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for overeating, by modulating the brain's stress response.

Phenolic acids (specifically, quercetin): They possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate conditions like metabolic syndrome associated with overeating, and also have a role in regulating blood sugar levels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for teas, as they are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which aid in digestion and help alleviate symptoms of overeating.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used to make teas that help reduce bloating and gas, due to their ability to relax the muscles in the digestive system.
Roots: The roots of the Ginkgo biloba plant are also used to make teas, as they contain compounds that help soothe digestive issues and promote a feeling of fullness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested leaves in a cool dark place for 7-10 days to remove moisture completely.
Chop the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 liter of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine mesh sieve and serve immediately while the tea is still warm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata teas helps with overeating because it contains compounds that aid in regulating appetite and metabolism. The tea's flavonoids and saponins have been shown to reduce cravings for unhealthy foods and promote feelings of fullness, making it easier to stick to a balanced diet. Additionally, Cassia auriculata has been traditionally used to support digestion and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to overeating and weight management issues. This herbal tea can be a useful tool in maintaining a healthy weight and lifestyle.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help regulate appetite and reduce food intake by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in carbohydrate metabolism.
Alkaloids: They exert a suppressive effect on appetite and weight gain by modulating neurotransmitters that control satiety and hunger.
Isoflavones: These compounds have been shown to reduce body weight and improve insulin sensitivity by modulating lipid metabolism and reducing fat accumulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cassia auriculata leaves are used to make teas for overeating because they are believed to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms associated with indigestion.
Seeds: Cassia auriculata seeds are used to make teas for overeating because they contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort.
Leaves: Cassia auriculata leaves are also used to make teas for overeating because they are thought to possess carminative properties, helping to ease bloating and gas associated with overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried cassia auriculata flowers from a trusted source on a sunny day.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the ground powder with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has reduced to your desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids before serving hot or cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ilex paraguariensis teas helps with overeating because it has been traditionally used to aid in weight management and appetite regulation. The caffeine content in Yerba Mate, derived from Ilex paraguariensis, acts as a natural appetite suppressant, reducing cravings for unhealthy foods. Additionally, its thermogenic properties increase metabolism, helping the body burn calories more efficiently. This combination of effects enables individuals to make healthier food choices and maintain a balanced diet, ultimately leading to reduced overeating and a more sustainable weight loss.
remedy_constituents: Caffeine: Stimulates metabolism and suppresses appetite by increasing the activity of neurotransmitters that regulate hunger and satiety.
Quercetin: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound that may help reduce inflammation associated with overeating and promote a healthy gut microbiome.
Theobromine: Slows down the digestion process and reduces the absorption of glucose from the gut, which may help regulate blood sugar levels and prevent overeating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for teas to help reduce overeating due to its potential to regulate appetite and metabolism.
Barks: Used for teas to help with weight management and reduce appetite due to its potential to suppress hunger.
Leaves: Used for teas to help with overeating as they may aid in digestion and reduce cravings for unhealthy foods.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from mature ilex paraguariensis trees during the early morning hours when dew is still present.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly to remove any dirt or impurities from the leaves.
Steep one teaspoon of dried leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves and any sediment from the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool completely before serving and consuming the herbal beverage immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum teas helps with overeating because it contains silymarin, a flavonoid compound that supports liver health. An overactive liver can lead to poor digestion and increased hunger, contributing to overeating. By promoting liver function, Silybum marianum tea may help regulate appetite and metabolism. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may reduce inflammation associated with overeating, promoting a healthier gut and improving overall digestive balance, which can lead to more mindful eating and a reduction in excessive food consumption.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin helps with overeating by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which can aid in weight management and improve overall liver function.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids help with overeating by regulating appetite and metabolism, reducing fat accumulation, and improving insulin sensitivity, all of which can contribute to weight loss.

Iridoids: Iridoids help with overeating by inhibiting the production of enzymes that break down carbohydrates, reducing the absorption of glucose, and slowing down the digestion of food, which can help with weight management.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make teas for overeating as they contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that may help alleviate digestive issues.
Leaves: They are used to make teas for overeating as they are believed to have a soothing effect on the digestive system and promote digestion.
Flowers: They are used to make teas for overeating as they may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum seeds and flowers, choosing fresh and organic material for tea production.
Dry the harvested silybum marianum material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried silybum marianum seeds and flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground silybum marianum powder with 1 quart of boiling water, steeping for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the silybum marianum tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup, serving immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis teas helps with overeating because of its unique combination of compounds and benefits. The tea's catechins and L-theanine promote a feeling of fullness and satisfaction, reducing the urge to overindulge. Additionally, the tea's ability to regulate appetite hormones, such as ghrelin and leptin, can help maintain a healthy weight. The calming and balancing effects of Camellia sinensis teas also support a more mindful eating approach, making it easier to recognize and control hunger cues. This results in a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Help with overeating by inhibiting the release of ghrelin, a hormone that stimulates appetite, and increasing the release of leptin, a hormone that suppresses appetite.
Theaflavins: Help with overeating by modulating gut microbiota and reducing inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to overeating behaviors.

Flavonoids: Help with overeating by reducing the expression of genes involved in fat storage and promoting the expression of genes involved in fat oxidation, leading to a reduction in food intake and weight gain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Camellia sinensis leaves are the most commonly used part to make teas for overeating because they contain a high amount of catechins and theaflavins, which help in regulating appetite and metabolism.
Buds: Camellia sinensis buds are used to make teas for overeating due to their high content of theanine, which can help in suppressing appetite and promoting weight loss.

Seeds: Camellia sinensis seeds are sometimes used in teas for overeating because they contain a compound called sinensisin, which has been shown to have potential in reducing fat absorption and aiding in weight management.
remedy_recipe: Select 25 grams of high-quality camellia sinensis leaves with a light brown color and delicate aroma.
Rinse the leaves in a fine-mesh strainer under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Gently pat the leaves dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the leaves in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 3 to 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and serve immediately after the steeping time has elapsed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Garcinia cambogia teas helps with overeating because it contains hydroxycitric acid (HCA), a compound that suppresses appetite and increases feelings of fullness. This natural ingredient blocks the production of citrate lyase, an enzyme that converts carbohydrates into fat, thereby reducing calorie absorption. By regulating metabolism and curbing hunger, Garcinia cambogia teas promote a healthier relationship with food, encouraging individuals to make more mindful eating choices and maintain a balanced weight. This results in a reduction in overeating and a more sustainable lifestyle.
remedy_constituents: Hydroxycitric acid (HCA): HCA inhibits the enzyme citrate lyase, which is responsible for converting carbohydrates into fat, thereby reducing fat production and helping to suppress appetite.
Malabaric acid: Malabaric acid has been shown to have appetite-suppressing properties, potentially by affecting the brain's reward centers and reducing cravings for food.

Flavonoids (specifically naringenin): Flavonoids like naringenin have been found to have anti-obesity effects by modulating lipid metabolism and improving insulin sensitivity, which can help with weight management and reduce overeating.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The most commonly used part of Garcinia cambogia is its fruit, specifically the rind or peel, which contains a high concentration of hydroxycitric acid (HCA), a key compound in managing overeating and weight loss.
Leaves: Garcinia cambogia leaves are also used to make teas, as they contain a lower concentration of HCA compared to the fruit but still exhibit some appetite-suppressing properties.
Roots: Some traditional herbal remedies use Garcinia cambogia roots to make teas, although they are less commonly used and studied compared to the fruit and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of garcinia cambogia fruit rinds from mature plants in a sunny location.
Dry the fruit rinds in a single layer at 150°f for 2 hours.
Crush the dried garcinia cambogia fruit rinds into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of garcinia cambogia powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve hot or iced immediately after preparation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ilex aquifolium teas helps with overeating because it contains compounds that stimulate digestion and boost metabolism, reducing the likelihood of overindulgence. The tea's antioxidants also help to balance blood sugar levels and curb cravings for unhealthy snacks. Furthermore, Ilex aquifolium has been traditionally used to aid in weight management by reducing inflammation and promoting a sense of fullness, making it easier to stick to a balanced diet and maintain a healthy weight. remedy_constituents: Quinic acid derivatives: These compounds help reduce food intake and decrease the desire to overeat by regulating gut hormones that control appetite.
Alkaloids (including berberine, aquifolin, and ibotenic acid): These alkaloids have been found to have a suppressive effect on appetite, reducing food intake and potentially aiding in weight loss.

Triterpenoids (including ursolic acid): These compounds have been shown to have anti-obesity properties by inhibiting the formation of new fat cells and reducing the storage of fat in existing fat cells, which can help with weight management.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ilex aquifolium leaves are used to make teas for overeating as they are believed to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion.
Roots: Ilex aquifolium roots are used to make teas for overeating as they are thought to help reduce inflammation in the digestive system and alleviate discomfort.
Buds: Ilex aquifolium buds are used to make teas for overeating as they are believed to have a calming effect on the digestive system and help with food stagnation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the ilex aquifolium leaves in late summer or early autumn when they are at their most potent state.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris that may be present.
Dry the leaves in a warm dark place for 2 weeks or until they are crumbly to ensure longevity.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried ilex aquifolium leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its properties.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups per day to experience its potential health benefits within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina teas helps with overeating because they are known to regulate appetite and metabolism. The herbal remedy contains alkaloids that help to reduce cravings for food and promote feelings of fullness. By reducing stress and anxiety, which often lead to overeating, Rauvolfia serpentina teas support a balanced diet and healthy weight management. remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds help with overeating by reducing appetite and promoting feelings of fullness, which can lead to a decrease in food intake.
Terpenoids: These compounds have been shown to inhibit the release of ghrelin, a hormone that stimulates appetite, thereby reducing overeating.
Indole alkaloids: These compounds have been found to have anxiolytic properties, which can help reduce stress-related eating and overeating behaviors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Rauvolfia serpentina due to their high alkaloid content, which is believed to help reduce appetite and prevent overeating.
Leaves: The leaves are also used in teas to help with overeating, as they are thought to have a similar effect to the roots in reducing hunger and promoting weight loss.
Seeds: The seeds of Rauvolfia serpentina are sometimes used in teas for overeating, although their effectiveness may be less pronounced than the roots or leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of rauvolfia serpentina roots and wash them thoroughly with clean water for 10 minutes.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Boil 250 milliliters of water in a saucepan for 5 minutes then reduce heat to a simmer.
Add the chopped roots to the boiling water and let them steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup for consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with overeating because its active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been found to regulate appetite and metabolism. The tea may also have a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and promoting a sense of fullness. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been traditionally used to support liver function, which is essential for breaking down fat and managing weight. As a result, incorporating Glycyrrhiza glabra tea into one's diet may help promote weight loss and reduce overeating tendencies.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Saponins: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can alleviate symptoms of overeating such as bloating and discomfort.
Glycyrrhizin: This saponin has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe the stomach lining, reducing the risk of stomach upset and nausea associated with overeating.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant properties and can help reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to feelings of overeating and weight gain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in the roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra, is used to make teas for overeating, as it helps reduce water retention and alleviates symptoms of bloating.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce water retention and alleviate symptoms associated with overeating.
Roots: Some herbal teas made from the roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to help alleviate symptoms of overeating, such as bloating, by regulating the body's fluid balance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a high quality root quality for best results.
Wash the dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in cold water to remove impurities and dust particles.
Dry the washed glycyrrhiza glabra roots at 60 degree celsius for 2 hours to retain flavor.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the herbal tea and let it cool down for 10 minutes before consumption always.
